document.source_title: Memorial Day 2026: Date, history, meaning, origins — What to know about the federal holiday

claims.claim_text: Memorial Day 2026 will be observed on May 25.
claims.evidence_text: In 2026, Memorial Day falls on Monday (May 25).

claims.claim_text: Memorial Day is a federal holiday.
claims.evidence_text: Memorial Day is an official federal holiday in the United States.

claims.claim_text: Memorial Day honors American military personnel who died while serving.
claims.evidence_text: Memorial Day is dedicated to honoring members of the US military who died while serving the country during wars, military conflicts and national missions.

entities.name: Memorial Day
entities.description: A federal holiday in the United States honoring military personnel who died in service.
entities.evidence_text: Memorial Day is dedicated to honoring members of the US military who died while serving the country during wars, military conflicts and national missions.

entities.name: Decoration Day
entities.description: The original name of Memorial Day, established to honor fallen soldiers.
entities.evidence_text: Originally known as Decoration Day, Memorial Day has evolved into a nationwide tribute.

entities.name: Arlington National Cemetery
entities.description: A military cemetery in Virginia where one of the earliest large-scale observances of Memorial Day took place.
entities.evidence_text: One of the earliest large-scale observances took place at Arlington National Cemetery in Virginia.

events.evidence_text: Memorial Day 2026 will be observed across the United States on Monday (May 25).
In 1868, General John A. Logan, leader of a Union veterans’ organization, officially proclaimed a national day of remembrance.

timelines.evidence_text: In 1967, Memorial Day officially became the recognized legal name of the holiday.
